definition: A sequence of data processing steps
example: The ETL pipeline processes the data nightly.
author: Admin

definition: A structured collection of data
example: The dataset contains 10,000 rows.
author: Admin

definition: A representation of data used for predictions
example: We trained a linear regression model.
author: Admin

definition: How well a system or model works
example: Model performance improved after tuning.
author: Admin

definition: The percentage of correct predictions
example: The model reached 95% accuracy.
author: Admin

definition: Time delay in a system
example: The API has a latency of 200ms.
author: Admin

definition: Able to handle growing amounts of work
example: We built a scalable microservice architecture.
author: Admin

definition: Improve performance or efficiency
example: We optimized the query to run faster.
author: Admin

definition: A step-by-step procedure for calculations
example: Sorting algorithms vary in efficiency.
author: Admin

definition: A request for data from a database
example: We wrote a SQL query to fetch users.
author: Admin

definition: A data structure to speed up lookups
example: Adding an index improved query performance.
author: Admin

definition: A block of reusable code
example: We defined a function to calculate sums.
author: Admin

definition: A sequence of instructions that repeats
example: We used a for-loop to iterate over items.
author: Admin

definition: A named storage for data in a program
example: The variable stores the user input.
author: Admin

definition: An instance of a class
example: We created an object of the User class.
author: Admin

definition: A blueprint for objects in OOP
example: The class defines properties and methods.
author: Admin

definition: A function defined in a class
example: The method calculates the user's age.
author: Admin

definition: An error that occurs during program execution
example: We handled the exception using try-except.
author: Admin

definition: A lightweight unit of execution
example: Each thread runs a separate task.
author: Admin

definition: An endpoint for sending or receiving data
example: We opened a socket to communicate between servers.
author: Admin

definition: A set of rules for communication
example: HTTP is a common protocol for web communication.
author: Admin

definition: The process of encoding data to prevent unauthorized access
example: We used encryption to protect user passwords.
author: Admin

definition: The process of prioritizing tasks or issues based on urgency and importance.
example: The support team uses triage to handle incoming bug reports.
author: Admin

definition: The process of splitting text or data into smaller units called tokens for processing.
example: Tokenization is the first step in most natural language processing pipelines.
author: Admin

definition: A unit of data used in computing, often representing a word, character, or authentication credential.
example: The API requires a valid access token for authentication.
author: Admin
